reasoning: The reference class is major foreign exchange pair short-to-medium term predictions. Base rates for forex pairs typically favor a random walk, placing long-horizon unforecastable paths near 0.50 probability of increasing. The FRED data series shows recent downward momentum, dropping to 146.9 by August 29, 2025. The FRED combined short-term statistical estimate for an increase is 0.47, reflecting both this negative momentum and historical seasonal traits. Additionally, analyst forecasts collected via search (from ING, Bank of America, etc.) widely project USD/JPY to trade somewhat lower by the end of 2025 (in the 136-145 range) due to the narrowing interest rate differential driven by anticipated BOJ rate hikes and Federal Reserve rate cuts. These combined factors give a slightly bearish outlook on the pair. Following the explicit instruction that uncertainty should increase with horizon (probabilities should move closer to 0.50 for distant dates), I project an initial probability of 0.44 for the 1-week horizon, which smoothly regresses to 0.49 for the 6-month horizon as accumulated variance masks the macro drift.
